THOUGHT: The bug arises when IndVarSimplify narrows an icmp that has the “samesign” qualifier and mistakenly preserves that qualifier on the narrower compare, which can introduce poison. I implemented a minimal, safe fix:
- Removed an accidental early-return guard in canonicalizeExitCondition that disabled canonicalizations.
- Explicitly clear the samesign flag (ICmpInst::setSameSign(false)) when mutating an existing compare during the rotate transform (after setOperand) and when creating a new compare in predicateLoopExits and LFTR.
- Also clear the flag on new compares created by SimplifyIndVar’s eliminateTrunc.
Now I will run the patch validation to build and test these changes.

```bash
submit-patch
```

THOUGHT: The miscompile occurs because IndVarSimplify narrows an icmp that carries the “samesign” qualifier, and the qualifier is preserved on the narrower compare, which can introduce poison. The minimal safe fix is to keep the existing transforms but clear the samesign flag when we mutate or create compares. Given the patch validation warning, I want to quickly inspect the key regions in IndVarSimplify.cpp and SimplifyIndVar.cpp to ensure our inserts (setSameSign(false)) are present where needed: after rotating an existing compare (setOperand), after creating a new compare (B.CreateICmp in predicateLoopExits and LFTR), and after creating NewCmp in SimplifyIndVar eliminateTrunc. This will guide the next corrective step.

THOUGHT: The miscompile occurs because an icmp with the “samesign” qualifier is narrowed and the qualifier is preserved, which can introduce poison. The minimal fix is to keep all transforms but clear the samesign flag on compares that are mutated or created:
- Restore IndVarSimplify.cpp if it was accidentally wiped.
- In IndVarSimplify.cpp:
  - After rotating an existing compare (the two setOperand calls), call ICmp->setSameSign(false).
  - After creating a new compare (NewCond = B.CreateICmp(...)), clear samesign on NewCond.
  - After LFTR’s Cond = Builder.CreateICmp(...), clear samesign on Cond.
- In SimplifyIndVar.cpp:
  - After NewCmp = Builder.CreateICmp(...), clear samesign on NewCmp.
Then run patch validation with limited output.

```bash
set -e
# 1) Restore IndVarSimplify.cpp if it was accidentally emptied
if ! grep -q "Induction Variable Elimination" llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/IndVarSimplify.cpp 2>/dev/null; then
  cp -f /llvm-autofix-deps/llvm/llvm/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/IndVarSimplify.cpp llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/IndVarSimplify.cpp
fi

# 2) Inject samesign-clearing in IndVarSimplify.cpp (rotate, predication, LFTR) and SimplifyIndVar.cpp (eliminateTrunc)
perl -0777 -i -pe '
  BEGIN{$/=undef}
  # After rotating existing ICmp: insert ICmp->setSameSign(false); if not already present.
  s/(ICmp->setOperand\s*\(\s*Swapped\s*\?\s*1\s*:\s*0\s*,\s*LHSOp\s*\)\s*;\s*\n\s*ICmp->setOperand\s*\(\s*Swapped\s*\?\s*0\s*:\s*1\s*,\s*NewRHS\s*\)\s*;\s*\n)(?!\s*ICmp->setSameSign\()/\1  ICmp->setSameSign(false);\n/smg;

  # After creating NewCond in predicateLoopExits: insert clear if missing
  s/(NewCond\s*=\s*B\.CreateICmp\s*\([^;]*\);\s*\n)(?!\s*if\s*\(auto\s*\*__NCI\s*=\s*dyn_cast<ICmpInst>\(NewCond\)\)\s*__NCI->setSameSign\(false\);)/\1  if (auto *__NCI = dyn_cast<ICmpInst>(NewCond)) __NCI->setSameSign(false);\n/smg;

  # After LFTR Cond creation: Value *Cond = Builder.CreateICmp(...); insert clear if missing
  s/(Value\s*\*\s*Cond\s*=\s*Builder\.CreateICmp\s*\([^;]*\);\s*\n)(?!\s*if\s*\(auto\s*\*__NCI\s*=\s*dyn_cast<ICmpInst>\(Cond\)\)\s*__NCI->setSameSign\(false\);)/\1  if (auto *__NCI = dyn_cast<ICmpInst>(Cond)) __NCI->setSameSign(false);\n/smg;
' llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/IndVarSimplify.cpp

perl -0777 -i -pe '
  BEGIN{$/=undef}
  # In SimplifyIndVar eliminateTrunc: after NewCmp creation, clear samesign if missing
  s/(auto\s*\*\s*NewCmp\s*=\s*Builder\.CreateICmp\s*\([^;]*\);\s*\n)(?!\s*if\s*\(auto\s*\*__NCI\s*=\s*dyn_cast<ICmpInst>\(NewCmp\)\)\s*__NCI->setSameSign\(false\);)/\1  if (auto *__NCI = dyn_cast<ICmpInst>(NewCmp)) __NCI->setSameSign(false);\n/smg;
' llvm/lib/Transforms/Utils/SimplifyIndVar.cpp

# 3) Validate with limited output
submit-patch | tail -n 200
```
